Psalm 104:1, 24, 29-30, 31, 34.text: Blesse the Lord, O my soule, O Lord my God, thou art very great: thou art clothed with honour and maiestie. O Lord, how manifold are thy workes! in wisedome hast thou made them all: the earth is full of thy riches. Thou hidest thy face, they are troubled, thou takest away their breath, they die: and returne to their dust. Thou sendest forth thy spirit, they are created: and thou renewest the face of the earth. The glory of the Lord shall endure for euer: the Lord shall reioyce in his workes. My meditation of him shalbe sweete: I will be glad in the Lord.
verses: Blesse the Lord, O my soule, O Lord my God, thou art very great: thou art clothed with honour and maiestie.
O Lord, how manifold are thy workes! in wisedome hast thou made them all: the earth is full of thy riches.
Thou hidest thy face, they are troubled, thou takest away their breath, they die: and returne to their dust.
Thou sendest forth thy spirit, they are created: and thou renewest the face of the earth.
The glory of the Lord shall endure for euer: the Lord shall reioyce in his workes.
My meditation of him shalbe sweete: I will be glad in the Lord.

Psalm 105:1-2, 3-4, 6-7, 8-9.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, call vpon his name: make knowen his deeds among the people. Sing vnto him; sing Psalmes vnto him: talke yee of all his wondrous workes. Glory yee in his holy name: let the heart of them reioyce, that seeke the Lord. Seeke the Lord, and his strength: seeke his face euermore. O yee seede of Abraham his seruant: yee children of Iacob his chosen. He is the Lord our God: his iudgements are in all the earth. He hath remembred his couenant for euer: the word which he commanded to a thousand generations. Which couenant he made with Abraham, and his oath vnto Isaac:
verses: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, call vpon his name: make knowen his deeds among the people.
Sing vnto him; sing Psalmes vnto him: talke yee of all his wondrous workes.
Glory yee in his holy name: let the heart of them reioyce, that seeke the Lord.
Seeke the Lord, and his strength: seeke his face euermore.
O yee seede of Abraham his seruant: yee children of Iacob his chosen.
He is the Lord our God: his iudgements are in all the earth.
He hath remembred his couenant for euer: the word which he commanded to a thousand generations.
Which couenant he made with Abraham, and his oath vnto Isaac:

Psalm 105:16-17, 18-19, 20-21.text: Moreouer hee called for a famine vpon the land: he brake the whole staffe of bread. Hee sent a man before them: euen Ioseph, who was sold for a seruant. Whose feete they hurt with fetters: he was layd in iron. Untill the time that his word came: the word of the Lord tried him. The king sent and loosed him: euen the ruler of the people, and let him goe free. Hee made him lord of his house: and ruler of all his substance:
verses: Moreouer hee called for a famine vpon the land: he brake the whole staffe of bread.
Hee sent a man before them: euen Ioseph, who was sold for a seruant.
Whose feete they hurt with fetters: he was layd in iron.
Untill the time that his word came: the word of the Lord tried him.
The king sent and loosed him: euen the ruler of the people, and let him goe free.
Hee made him lord of his house: and ruler of all his substance:

Psalm 105:2-3, 36-37, 42-43.text: Sing vnto him; sing Psalmes vnto him: talke yee of all his wondrous workes. Glory yee in his holy name: let the heart of them reioyce, that seeke the Lord. Hee smote also all the first borne in their land: the chiefe of all their strength. Hee brought them foorth also with siluer and gold: and there was not one feeble person among their tribes. For he remembred his holy promise: and Abraham his seruant. And he brought forth his people with ioy: and his chosen with gladnesse:
verses: Sing vnto him; sing Psalmes vnto him: talke yee of all his wondrous workes.
Glory yee in his holy name: let the heart of them reioyce, that seeke the Lord.
Hee smote also all the first borne in their land: the chiefe of all their strength.
Hee brought them foorth also with siluer and gold: and there was not one feeble person among their tribes.
For he remembred his holy promise: and Abraham his seruant.
And he brought forth his people with ioy: and his chosen with gladnesse:

Psalm 105:2-3, 4-5, 6-7.text: Sing vnto him; sing Psalmes vnto him: talke yee of all his wondrous workes. Glory yee in his holy name: let the heart of them reioyce, that seeke the Lord. Seeke the Lord, and his strength: seeke his face euermore. Remember his maruellous workes, that hee hath done: his wonders, and the iudgements of his mouth, O yee seede of Abraham his seruant: yee children of Iacob his chosen. He is the Lord our God: his iudgements are in all the earth.
verses: Sing vnto him; sing Psalmes vnto him: talke yee of all his wondrous workes.

Psalm 106:3-4, 35-36, 37 and 40.text: Blessed are they that keepe iudgement: and he that doeth righteousnesse at all times. Remember me, O Lord, with the fauour that thou bearest vnto thy people: O visite me with thy saluation: But were mingled among the heathen, and learned their workes. And they serued their idoles: which were a snare vnto them. Yea they sacrificed their sonnes, and their daughters vnto deuils, Therefore was the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people: insomuch that he abhorred his owne inheritance.
verses: Blessed are they that keepe iudgement: and he that doeth righteousnesse at all times.
Remember me, O Lord, with the fauour that thou bearest vnto thy people: O visite me with thy saluation:
But were mingled among the heathen, and learned their workes.
And they serued their idoles: which were a snare vnto them.
Yea they sacrificed their sonnes, and their daughters vnto deuils,
Therefore was the wrath of the Lord kindled against his people: insomuch that he abhorred his owne inheritance.

Psalm 106:6-7ab, 19-20, 21-22.text: Wee haue sinned with our fathers: we haue committed iniquitie, we haue done wickedly. Our fathers vnderstood not thy wonders in Egypt, they remembred not the multitude of thy mercies: but prouoked him at the sea, euen at the Red-sea. They made a calfe in Horeb: and worshipped the molten image. Thus they changed their glory, into the similitude of an oxe that eateth grasse. They forgate God their Sauiour: which had done great things in Egypt: Wonderous workes in the lande of Ham: and terrible things by the red Sea.
verses: Wee haue sinned with our fathers: we haue committed iniquitie, we haue done wickedly.
Our fathers vnderstood not thy wonders in Egypt, they remembred not the multitude of thy mercies: but prouoked him at the sea, euen at the Red-sea.
They made a calfe in Horeb: and worshipped the molten image.
Thus they changed their glory, into the similitude of an oxe that eateth grasse.
They forgate God their Sauiour: which had done great things in Egypt:
Wonderous workes in the lande of Ham: and terrible things by the red Sea.

Psalm 107:2-3, 4-5, 6-7, 8-9.text: Let the redeemed of the Lord say so: whome he hath redeemed from the hand of the enemie: And gathered them out of the lands, from the East and from the West: from the North and from the South. They wandred in the wildernes, in a solitary way: they found no citie to dwell in. Hungry and thirstie: their soule fainted in them. Then they cryed vnto the Lord in their trouble: and he deliuered them out of their distresses. And hee led them forth by the right way: that they might goe to a citie of habitation. Oh that men would praise the Lord, for his goodnesse: and for his wonderfull workes to the children of men. For he satisfieth the longing soule: and filleth the hungry soule with goodnesse.
verses: Let the redeemed of the Lord say so: whome he hath redeemed from the hand of the enemie:
And gathered them out of the lands, from the East and from the West: from the North and from the South.
They wandred in the wildernes, in a solitary way: they found no citie to dwell in.
Hungry and thirstie: their soule fainted in them.
Then they cryed vnto the Lord in their trouble: and he deliuered them out of their distresses.
And hee led them forth by the right way: that they might goe to a citie of habitation.
Oh that men would praise the Lord, for his goodnesse: and for his wonderfull workes to the children of men.
For he satisfieth the longing soule: and filleth the hungry soule with goodnesse.

Psalm 10:1-2, 3-4, 7-8, 14.text: Why standest thou afarre off, O Lord? why hidest thou thy selfe in times of trouble? The wicked in his pride doeth persecute the poore: let them be taken in the deuices that they haue imagined. For the wicked boasteth of his hearts desire, and blesseth the couetous, whom the Lord abhorreth. The wicked through the pride of his countenance will not seeke after God: God is not in all his thoughts. His mouth is full of cursing, and deceit, and fraud: vnder his tongue is mischiefe and vanitie. He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doeth he murder the innocent: his eyes are priuily set against the poore. Thou hast seene it, for thou beholdest mischiefe and spite to requite it with thy hand: the poore committeth himselfe vnto thee, thou art the helper of the fatherlesse.
verses: Why standest thou afarre off, O Lord? why hidest thou thy selfe in times of trouble?
The wicked in his pride doeth persecute the poore: let them be taken in the deuices that they haue imagined.
For the wicked boasteth of his hearts desire, and blesseth the couetous, whom the Lord abhorreth.
The wicked through the pride of his countenance will not seeke after God: God is not in all his thoughts.
His mouth is full of cursing, and deceit, and fraud: vnder his tongue is mischiefe and vanitie.
He sitteth in the lurking places of the villages: in the secret places doeth he murder the innocent: his eyes are priuily set against the poore.
Thou hast seene it, for thou beholdest mischiefe and spite to requite it with thy hand: the poore committeth himselfe vnto thee, thou art the helper of the fatherlesse.

Psalm 110.text: The Lord said vnto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand: vntil I make thine enemies thy footestoole. The Lord shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies. Thy people shalbe willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holinesse from the wombe of the morning: thou hast the dew of thy youth. The Lord hath sworne, and will not repent, thou art a Priest for euer: after the order of Melchizedek. The Lord at thy right hand shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath. He shal iudge among the heathen, he shal fil the places with the dead bodies: he shall wound the heads ouer many countries. He shall drinke of the brooke in the way: therefore shall hee lift vp the head.
verses: The Lord said vnto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand: vntil I make thine enemies thy footestoole.
The Lord shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies.
Thy people shalbe willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holinesse from the wombe of the morning: thou hast the dew of thy youth.
The Lord hath sworne, and will not repent, thou art a Priest for euer: after the order of Melchizedek.
The Lord at thy right hand shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath.
He shal iudge among the heathen, he shal fil the places with the dead bodies: he shall wound the heads ouer many countries.
He shall drinke of the brooke in the way: therefore shall hee lift vp the head.

Psalm 111.text: Praise yee the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart: in the assembly of the vpright, and in the Congregation. The workes of the Lord are great: sought out of all them that haue pleasure therein. His worke is honourable and glorious: and his righteousnesse endureth for euer. Hee hath made his wonderfull works to be remembred: the Lord is gracious, and full of compassion. He hath giuen meate vnto them that feare him: he will euer be mindfull of his couenant. He hath shewed his people the power of his workes: that he may giue them the heritage of the heathen. The works of his hands are veritie and iudgment: all his commandements are sure. They stand fast for euer and euer: and are done in trueth and vprightnes. He sent redemption vnto his people, hee hath commanded his couenant for euer: holy and reuerend is his Name. The feare of the Lord is the beginning of wisedome, a good vnderstanding haue all they that doe his commandements: his praise endureth for euer.

Psalm 112.text: Praise ye the Lord. Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord, that delighteth greatly in his Commaundements. His seed shall bee mightie vpon earth: the generation of the vpright shalbe blessed. Wealth and riches shalbe in his house: and his righteousnesse endureth for euer. Unto the vpright there ariseth light in the darknesse: hee is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous. A good man sheweth fauour and lendeth: he will guide his affaires with discretion. Surely he shall not be moued for euer: the righteous shalbe in euerlasting remembrance. He shall not be afraid of euill tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord. His heart is established, hee shall not be afraid, vntill he see his desire vpon his enemies. He hath dispersed, he hath giuen to the poore: his righteousnesse endureth for euer; his horne shalbe exalted with honour. The wicked shall see it, and be grieued; he shall gnash with his teeth, and melt away: the desire of the wicked shall perish.
verses: Praise ye the Lord. Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord, that delighteth greatly in his Commaundements.
His seed shall bee mightie vpon earth: the generation of the vpright shalbe blessed.
Wealth and riches shalbe in his house: and his righteousnesse endureth for euer.
Unto the vpright there ariseth light in the darknesse: hee is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.

Psalm 113:1-2, 3-4, 5-6.text: Praise yee the Lord. Praise, O yee seruants of the Lord: praise the name of the Lord. Blessed be the name of the Lord: from this time forth and for euermore. From the rising of the sunne vnto the going downe of the same: the Lords name is to be praised. The Lord is high aboue all nations: and his glory aboue the heauens. Who is like vnto the Lord our God: who dwelleth on high: Who humbleth himselfe to behold the things that are in heauen, and in the earth?
verses: Praise yee the Lord. Praise, O yee seruants of the Lord: praise the name of the Lord.
Blessed be the name of the Lord: from this time forth and for euermore.
From the rising of the sunne vnto the going downe of the same: the Lords name is to be praised.
The Lord is high aboue all nations: and his glory aboue the heauens.
Who is like vnto the Lord our God: who dwelleth on high:
Who humbleth himselfe to behold the things that are in heauen, and in the earth?

Psalm 113:1-2, 4-6, 7-8.text: Praise yee the Lord. Praise, O yee seruants of the Lord: praise the name of the Lord. Blessed be the name of the Lord: from this time forth and for euermore. He raiseth vp the poore out of the dust: and lifteth the needie out of the dung-hill: That he may set him with princes: euen with the princes of his people.

Psalm 114.text: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Iacob from a people of strange language: Iudah was his sanctuarie: and Israel his dominion. The sea sawe it, and fled: Iordan was driuen backe. The mountaines skipped like rammes: and the little hilles like lambes. What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Iordan, that thou wast driuen backe? Yee mountaines, that yee skipped like rammes: and yee little hilles like lambes? Tremble thou earth at the presence of the Lord: at the presence of the God of Iacob: Which turned the rocke into a standing water: the flint into a fountaine of waters.
verses: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Iacob from a people of strange language:
Iudah was his sanctuarie: and Israel his dominion.
The sea sawe it, and fled: Iordan was driuen backe.
The mountaines skipped like rammes: and the little hilles like lambes.
What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Iordan, that thou wast driuen backe?
Yee mountaines, that yee skipped like rammes: and yee little hilles like lambes?
Tremble thou earth at the presence of the Lord: at the presence of the God of Iacob:
Which turned the rocke into a standing water: the flint into a fountaine of waters.

Psalm 115:1-2, 3-4, 15-16.text: Not vnto vs, O Lord, not vnto vs, but vnto thy name giue glory: for thy mercy, and for thy truthes sake. Wherefore should the heathen say: Where is now their God? But our God is in the heauens: he hath done whatsoeuer he pleased. Their idoles are siluer and gold: the worke of mens hands. You are blessed of the Lord: which made heauen and earth. The heauen, euen the heauens are the Lords: but the earth hath hee giuen to the children of men.
verses: Not vnto vs, O Lord, not vnto vs, but vnto thy name giue glory: for thy mercy, and for thy truthes sake.
Wherefore should the heathen say: Where is now their God?
But our God is in the heauens: he hath done whatsoeuer he pleased.
Their idoles are siluer and gold: the worke of mens hands.
You are blessed of the Lord: which made heauen and earth.
The heauen, euen the heauens are the Lords: but the earth hath hee giuen to the children of men.

Psalm 115:3-4, 5-6, 7ab-8, 9-10.text: But our God is in the heauens: he hath done whatsoeuer he pleased. Their idoles are siluer and gold: the worke of mens hands. They haue mouths, but they speake not; eies haue they, but they see not. They haue eares, but they heare not: noses haue they, but they smell not. They haue hands, but they handle not, feete haue they, but they walke not: neither speake they through their throat. They that make them are like vnto them: so is euery one that trusteth in them. O Israel, trust thou in the Lord: he is their helpe and their shield. O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord: he is their helpe & their shield.
verses: But our God is in the heauens: he hath done whatsoeuer he pleased.
Their idoles are siluer and gold: the worke of mens hands.
They haue mouths, but they speake not; eies haue they, but they see not.
They haue eares, but they heare not: noses haue they, but they smell not.
They haue hands, but they handle not, feete haue they, but they walke not: neither speake they through their throat.
They that make them are like vnto them: so is euery one that trusteth in them.
O Israel, trust thou in the Lord: he is their helpe and their shield.
O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord: he is their helpe & their shield.

Psalm 116:12-13, 14-17, 18-19.text: What shall I render vnto the Lord: for all his benefits towards mee? I will take the cup of saluation: and call vpon the Name of the Lord. I will pay my vowes vnto the Lord: now in the presence of all his people. Precious in þ sight of the Lord: is the death of his Saints. Oh Lord, truely I am thy seruant, I am thy seruant, and the sonne of thy handmayde: thou hast loosed my bonds. I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thankes-giuing: and will call vpon the Name of the Lord. I will pay my vowes vnto the Lord: now in the presence of all his people: In the Courts of the Lords house, in the middes of thee, O Ierusalem. Praise ye the Lord.
verses: What shall I render vnto the Lord: for all his benefits towards mee?
I will take the cup of saluation: and call vpon the Name of the Lord.
I will pay my vowes vnto the Lord: now in the presence of all his people.
Precious in þ sight of the Lord: is the death of his Saints.
Oh Lord, truely I am thy seruant, I am thy seruant, and the sonne of thy handmayde: thou hast loosed my bonds.
I will offer to thee the sacrifice of thankes-giuing: and will call vpon the Name of the Lord.
I will pay my vowes vnto the Lord: now in the presence of all his people:
In the Courts of the Lords house, in the middes of thee, O Ierusalem. Praise ye the Lord.

Psalm 117:1bc, 2.text: O praise the Lord, all ye nations: praise him all ye people. For his merciful kindnesse is great toward vs: and the trueth of the Lord endureth for euer. Praise ye the Lord.
verses: O praise the Lord, all ye nations: praise him all ye people.
For his merciful kindnesse is great toward vs: and the trueth of the Lord endureth for euer. Praise ye the Lord.

Psalm 118:1 and 14-15ab, 16-18, 19-21.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer. The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation. The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord. The Lord hath chastened me sore: but he hath not giuen me ouer vnto death. Open to mee the gates of righteousnesse: I will goe into them, and I will praise the Lord: This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter. I will praise thee, for thou hast heard mee: and art become my saluation.
verses: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer.
The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation.
The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord.
The Lord hath chastened me sore: but he hath not giuen me ouer vnto death.
Open to mee the gates of righteousnesse: I will goe into them, and I will praise the Lord:
This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter.
I will praise thee, for thou hast heard mee: and art become my saluation.

Psalm 118:1 and 8-9, 19-21, 25-27a.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer. It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in man. It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in Princes. Open to mee the gates of righteousnesse: I will goe into them, and I will praise the Lord: This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter. I will praise thee, for thou hast heard mee: and art become my saluation. Saue now, I beseech thee, O Lord: O Lord, I beseech thee, send now prosperitie. Blessed be he that commeth in the Name of the Lord: wee haue blessed you out of the house of the Lord. God is the Lord, which hath shewed vs light, bind the sacrifice with cords: euen vnto the horns of the Altar.

This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter.

Psalm 118:1-2 and 4, 22-24, 25-27a.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer. Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer. Let them now that feare the Lord, say: that his mercy endureth for euer. The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner. This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes. This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will reioyce, and be glad in it. Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
Let them now that feare the Lord, say: that his mercy endureth for euer.

This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes.
This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will reioyce, and be glad in it.
Saue now, I beseech thee, O Lord: O Lord, I beseech thee, send now prosperitie.

Psalm 118:1-2, 16-17, 22-23.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer. Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer. The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord. The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner. This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes.
verses: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer.
Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord.
The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner.
This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes.

Psalm 118:1-24.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer. Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer. Let the house of Aaron now say: that his mercy endureth for euer. Let them now that feare the Lord, say: that his mercy endureth for euer. I called vpon the Lord in distresse: the Lord answered me, and set me in a large place. The Lord is on my side, I will not feare: What can man doe vnto mee? The Lord taketh my part with them that helpe me: therfore shall I see my desire vpon them that hate me. It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in man. It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in Princes. All nations compassed me about: but in the Name of the Lord, will I destroy them. They compassed mee about, yea they compassed mee about: but in the Name of the Lord, I will destroy them. They compassed mee about like Bees, they are quenched as the fire of thornes: for in the Name of the Lord I wil destroy them. Thou hast thrust sore at mee that I might fall: but the Lord helped mee. The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation. The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord. The Lord hath chastened me sore: but he hath not giuen me ouer vnto death. Open to mee the gates of righteousnesse: I will goe into them, and I will praise the Lord: This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter. I will praise thee, for thou hast heard mee: and art become my saluation. The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner. This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes. This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will reioyce, and be glad in it.
verses: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer.
Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
Let the house of Aaron now say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
Let them now that feare the Lord, say: that his mercy endureth for euer.
I called vpon the Lord in distresse: the Lord answered me, and set me in a large place.
The Lord is on my side, I will not feare: What can man doe vnto mee?
The Lord taketh my part with them that helpe me: therfore shall I see my desire vpon them that hate me.
It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in man.
It is better to trust in the Lord: then to put confidence in Princes.
All nations compassed me about: but in the Name of the Lord, will I destroy them.
They compassed mee about, yea they compassed mee about: but in the Name of the Lord, I will destroy them.
They compassed mee about like Bees, they are quenched as the fire of thornes: for in the Name of the Lord I wil destroy them.
Thou hast thrust sore at mee that I might fall: but the Lord helped mee.
The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation.
The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord.
The Lord hath chastened me sore: but he hath not giuen me ouer vnto death.
Open to mee the gates of righteousnesse: I will goe into them, and I will praise the Lord:
This gate of the Lord: into which the righteous shall enter.
I will praise thee, for thou hast heard mee: and art become my saluation.
The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner.
This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes.
This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will reioyce, and be glad in it.

Psalm 118:1b-2, 16ab-17, 28.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: because his mercie endureth for euer. Let Israel now say: that his mercy endureth for euer. The Right hand of the Lord is exalted: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. I shall not die, but liue: and declare the workes of the Lord. Thou art my God, and I will praise thee: thou art my God, I will exalt thee.

The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation. The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly. Thou hast thrust sore at mee that I might fall: but the Lord helped mee.
The Lord is my strength and song: and is become my saluation.
The voice of reioycing and saluation is in the tabernacles of the righteous: the Right hand of the Lord doeth valiantly.
The stone which the builders refused: is become the head stone of the corner.
This is the Lords doing: it is marueilous in our eyes.
This is the day which the Lord hath made: we will reioyce, and be glad in it.

Psalm 119:1, 2, 10, 11, 17, 18.text: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord. Blessed are they that keepe his testimonies: and that seeke him with the whole heart. With my whole heart haue I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy Commandements. Thy word haue I hidde in mine heart: that I might not sinne against thee. Deale bountifully with thy seruant; that I may liue, and keepe thy word. Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wonderous things out of thy Law.
verses: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord.
Blessed are they that keepe his testimonies: and that seeke him with the whole heart.
With my whole heart haue I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy Commandements.
Thy word haue I hidde in mine heart: that I might not sinne against thee.
Deale bountifully with thy seruant; that I may liue, and keepe thy word.
Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wonderous things out of thy Law.

Psalm 119:1, 27, 30, 34, 35, 44.text: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord. Make me to vnderstand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talke of thy wonderous workes. I haue chosen the way of trueth: thy iudgements haue I laid before me. Giue me vnderstanding, and I shall keepe thy Law: yea I shall obserue it with my whole heart. Make me to goe in the path of thy commandements: for therein doe I delight. So shall I keepe thy Law continually: for euer and euer.
verses: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord.
Make me to vnderstand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talke of thy wonderous workes.
I haue chosen the way of trueth: thy iudgements haue I laid before me.
Giue me vnderstanding, and I shall keepe thy Law: yea I shall obserue it with my whole heart.
Make me to goe in the path of thy commandements: for therein doe I delight.
So shall I keepe thy Law continually: for euer and euer.

Psalm 119:1-2, 4-5, 17-18, 33-34.text: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord. Blessed are they that keepe his testimonies: and that seeke him with the whole heart. Thou hast commaunded vs to keepe thy precepts diligently. O that my wayes were directed to keepe thy statutes! Deale bountifully with thy seruant; that I may liue, and keepe thy word. Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wonderous things out of thy Law. Teach me, O Lord, the way of thy Statutes: and I shall keepe it vnto the end. Giue me vnderstanding, and I shall keepe thy Law: yea I shall obserue it with my whole heart.
verses: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord.
Blessed are they that keepe his testimonies: and that seeke him with the whole heart.
Thou hast commaunded vs to keepe thy precepts diligently.
O that my wayes were directed to keepe thy statutes!
Deale bountifully with thy seruant; that I may liue, and keepe thy word.
Open thou mine eyes, that I may behold wonderous things out of thy Law.
Teach me, O Lord, the way of thy Statutes: and I shall keepe it vnto the end.
Giue me vnderstanding, and I shall keepe thy Law: yea I shall obserue it with my whole heart.

Psalm 119:1-2, 4-5, 7-8.text: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord. Blessed are they that keepe his testimonies: and that seeke him with the whole heart. Thou hast commaunded vs to keepe thy precepts diligently. O that my wayes were directed to keepe thy statutes! I will praise thee with vprightnesse of heart: when I shall haue learned thy righteous iudgements. I will keepe thy statutes: O forsake me not vtterly.
verses: Blessed are the vndefiled in the way: who walke in the Law of the Lord.
Blessed are they that keepe his testimonies: and that seeke him with the whole heart.
Thou hast commaunded vs to keepe thy precepts diligently.
O that my wayes were directed to keepe thy statutes!
I will praise thee with vprightnesse of heart: when I shall haue learned thy righteous iudgements.
I will keepe thy statutes: O forsake me not vtterly.

Psalm 119:14, 24, 72, 103, 111, 131.text: I haue reioyced in the way of thy testimonies: as much as in all riches. Thy testimonies also are my delight: and my counsellers. The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer. How sweet are thy words vnto my taste! yea, sweeter then hony to my mouth. Thy Testimonies haue I taken as an heritage for euer: for they are the reioycing of my heart. I opened my mouth, and panted: for I longed for thy Commandements.
verses: I haue reioyced in the way of thy testimonies: as much as in all riches.
Thy testimonies also are my delight: and my counsellers.
The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer.
How sweet are thy words vnto my taste! yea, sweeter then hony to my mouth.
Thy Testimonies haue I taken as an heritage for euer: for they are the reioycing of my heart.
I opened my mouth, and panted: for I longed for thy Commandements.

Psalm 119:157, 160, 161, 165, 166, 168.text: Many are my persecutors, and mine enemies: yet doe I not decline from thy testimonies. Thy word is true from the beginning: and euery one of thy righteous iudgements endureth for euer. Princes haue persecuted mee without a cause: but my heart standeth in awe of thy word. Great peace haue they which loue thy law: & nothing shall offend them. Lord, I haue hoped for thy saluation: and done thy commandements. I haue kept thy precepts and thy testimonies: for all my wayes are before thee.
verses: Many are my persecutors, and mine enemies: yet doe I not decline from thy testimonies.
Thy word is true from the beginning: and euery one of thy righteous iudgements endureth for euer.
Princes haue persecuted mee without a cause: but my heart standeth in awe of thy word.
Great peace haue they which loue thy law: & nothing shall offend them.
Lord, I haue hoped for thy saluation: and done thy commandements.
I haue kept thy precepts and thy testimonies: for all my wayes are before thee.

Psalm 119:23-24, 26-27, 29-30.text: Princes also did sit and speake against me: but thy seruant did meditate in thy statutes. Thy testimonies also are my delight: and my counsellers. I haue declared my wayes, and thou heardest me: teach me thy Statutes. Make me to vnderstand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talke of thy wonderous workes. Remoue from mee the way of lying: and grant me thy Law graciously. I haue chosen the way of trueth: thy iudgements haue I laid before me.
verses: Princes also did sit and speake against me: but thy seruant did meditate in thy statutes.
Thy testimonies also are my delight: and my counsellers.
I haue declared my wayes, and thou heardest me: teach me thy Statutes.
Make me to vnderstand the way of thy precepts: so shall I talke of thy wonderous workes.
Remoue from mee the way of lying: and grant me thy Law graciously.
I haue chosen the way of trueth: thy iudgements haue I laid before me.

Psalm 119:29, 43, 79, 80, 95, 102.text: Remoue from mee the way of lying: and grant me thy Law graciously. And take not the word of trueth vtterly out of my mouth: for I haue hoped in thy iudgements. Let those that feare thee turne vnto me: and those that haue knowen thy testimonies. Let my heart be sound in thy statutes; that I be not ashamed. The wicked haue waited for me to destroy me: but I will consider thy testimonies. I haue not departed from thy Iudgements: for thou hast taught me.
verses: Remoue from mee the way of lying: and grant me thy Law graciously.
And take not the word of trueth vtterly out of my mouth: for I haue hoped in thy iudgements.
Let those that feare thee turne vnto me: and those that haue knowen thy testimonies.
Let my heart be sound in thy statutes; that I be not ashamed.
The wicked haue waited for me to destroy me: but I will consider thy testimonies.
I haue not departed from thy Iudgements: for thou hast taught me.

Psalm 119:29, 72, 89, 101, 104, 163.text: Remoue from mee the way of lying: and grant me thy Law graciously. The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer. Foreuer, O Lord, thy word is setled, in heauen. I haue refrained my feete from euery euill way: that I may keepe thy word. Through thy precepts I get vnderstanding: therefore I hate euery false way. I hate and abhorre lying: but thy Law doe I loue.
verses: Remoue from mee the way of lying: and grant me thy Law graciously.
The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer.
Foreuer, O Lord, thy word is setled, in heauen.
I haue refrained my feete from euery euill way: that I may keepe thy word.
Through thy precepts I get vnderstanding: therefore I hate euery false way.
I hate and abhorre lying: but thy Law doe I loue.

Psalm 119:41, 43, 44, 45, 47, 48.text: Let thy mercies come also vnto me, O Lord: euen thy saluation, according to thy word. And take not the word of trueth vtterly out of my mouth: for I haue hoped in thy iudgements. So shall I keepe thy Law continually: for euer and euer. And I wil walke at libertie: for I seeke thy precepts. And I will delight my selfe in thy commandements, which I haue loued. My hands also will I lift vp vnto thy commandements, which I haue loued: and I will meditate in thy statutes.
verses: Let thy mercies come also vnto me, O Lord: euen thy saluation, according to thy word.
And take not the word of trueth vtterly out of my mouth: for I haue hoped in thy iudgements.
So shall I keepe thy Law continually: for euer and euer.
And I wil walke at libertie: for I seeke thy precepts.
And I will delight my selfe in thy commandements, which I haue loued.
My hands also will I lift vp vnto thy commandements, which I haue loued: and I will meditate in thy statutes.

Psalm 119:53, 61, 134, 150, 155, 158.text: Horrour hath taken holde vpon me, because of the wicked that forsake thy Law. The bands of the wicked haue robbed me: but I haue not forgotten thy lawe. Deliuer me from the oppression of man: so will I keepe thy precepts. They draw nigh that follow after mischiefe: they are farre from thy Law. Saluation is farre from the wicked: for they seeke not thy statutes. I beheld the transgressours, and was grieued: because they kept not thy word.
verses: Horrour hath taken holde vpon me, because of the wicked that forsake thy Law.
The bands of the wicked haue robbed me: but I haue not forgotten thy lawe.
Deliuer me from the oppression of man: so will I keepe thy precepts.
They draw nigh that follow after mischiefe: they are farre from thy Law.
Saluation is farre from the wicked: for they seeke not thy statutes.
I beheld the transgressours, and was grieued: because they kept not thy word.

Psalm 119:57, 72, 76-77, 127-128, 129-13.text: Thou art my portion, O Lord, I haue said, that I would keepe thy words. The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer. Let, I pray thee, thy mercifull kindnesse be for my comfort; according to thy word vnto thy seruant. Let thy tender mercies come vnto me, that I may liue: for thy lawe is my delight. Therefore I loue thy Commandements: aboue gold, yea aboue fine gold. Therefore I esteeme all thy precepts concerning all things to be right: and I hate euery false way. Thy Testimonies are wonderfull: therefore doeth my soule keepe them. The entrance of thy wordes giueth light: it giueth vnderstanding vnto the simple.
verses: Thou art my portion, O Lord, I haue said, that I would keepe thy words.
The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer.
Let, I pray thee, thy mercifull kindnesse be for my comfort; according to thy word vnto thy seruant.
Let thy tender mercies come vnto me, that I may liue: for thy lawe is my delight.
Therefore I loue thy Commandements: aboue gold, yea aboue fine gold.
Therefore I esteeme all thy precepts concerning all things to be right: and I hate euery false way.
Thy Testimonies are wonderfull: therefore doeth my soule keepe them.
The entrance of thy wordes giueth light: it giueth vnderstanding vnto the simple.

Psalm 119:66, 68, 76, 77, 93, 94.text: Teach me good iudgement and knowledge: for I haue beleeued thy commandements. Thou art good, and doest good; teach me thy statutes. Let, I pray thee, thy mercifull kindnesse be for my comfort; according to thy word vnto thy seruant. Let thy tender mercies come vnto me, that I may liue: for thy lawe is my delight. I will neuer forget thy precepts: for with them thou hast quickened me. I am thine, saue me: for I haue sought thy precepts.
verses: Teach me good iudgement and knowledge: for I haue beleeued thy commandements.
Thou art good, and doest good; teach me thy statutes.
Let, I pray thee, thy mercifull kindnesse be for my comfort; according to thy word vnto thy seruant.
Let thy tender mercies come vnto me, that I may liue: for thy lawe is my delight.
I will neuer forget thy precepts: for with them thou hast quickened me.
I am thine, saue me: for I haue sought thy precepts.

Psalm 119:66, 71, 75, 91, 125, 130.text: Teach me good iudgement and knowledge: for I haue beleeued thy commandements. It is good for me that I haue bene afflicted: that I might learne thy statutes. I knowe, O Lord, that thy iudgements are right: and that thou in faithfulnesse hast afflicted me. They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all are thy seruants. I am thy seruant, giue me vnderstanding: that I may know thy Testimonies. The entrance of thy wordes giueth light: it giueth vnderstanding vnto the simple.
verses: Teach me good iudgement and knowledge: for I haue beleeued thy commandements.
It is good for me that I haue bene afflicted: that I might learne thy statutes.
I knowe, O Lord, that thy iudgements are right: and that thou in faithfulnesse hast afflicted me.
They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all are thy seruants.
I am thy seruant, giue me vnderstanding: that I may know thy Testimonies.
The entrance of thy wordes giueth light: it giueth vnderstanding vnto the simple.

Psalm 119:67, 68, 71, 72, 75, 76.text: Before I was afflicted, I went astray: but now haue I kept thy word. Thou art good, and doest good; teach me thy statutes. It is good for me that I haue bene afflicted: that I might learne thy statutes. The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer. I knowe, O Lord, that thy iudgements are right: and that thou in faithfulnesse hast afflicted me. Let, I pray thee, thy mercifull kindnesse be for my comfort; according to thy word vnto thy seruant.
verses: Before I was afflicted, I went astray: but now haue I kept thy word.
Thou art good, and doest good; teach me thy statutes.
It is good for me that I haue bene afflicted: that I might learne thy statutes.
The law of thy mouth is better vnto me: then thousands of gold and siluer.
I knowe, O Lord, that thy iudgements are right: and that thou in faithfulnesse hast afflicted me.
Let, I pray thee, thy mercifull kindnesse be for my comfort; according to thy word vnto thy seruant.

Psalm 119:89, 90, 91, 130, 135, 175.text: Foreuer, O Lord, thy word is setled, in heauen. Thy faithfulnesse is vnto all generations: thou hast established the earth, and it abideth. They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all are thy seruants. The entrance of thy wordes giueth light: it giueth vnderstanding vnto the simple. Make thy face to shine vpon thy seruant: and teach me thy Statutes. Let my soule liue, and it shall praise thee: and let thy iudgments helpe me.
verses: Foreuer, O Lord, thy word is setled, in heauen.
Thy faithfulnesse is vnto all generations: thou hast established the earth, and it abideth.
They continue this day according to thine ordinances: for all are thy seruants.
The entrance of thy wordes giueth light: it giueth vnderstanding vnto the simple.
Make thy face to shine vpon thy seruant: and teach me thy Statutes.
Let my soule liue, and it shall praise thee: and let thy iudgments helpe me.

Psalm 119:9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14.text: Wherewithall shall a yong man cleanse his way? by taking heede thereto according to thy word. With my whole heart haue I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy Commandements. Thy word haue I hidde in mine heart: that I might not sinne against thee. Blessed art thou, O Lord: teach me thy statutes. With my lips haue I declared all the iudgements of thy mouth. I haue reioyced in the way of thy testimonies: as much as in all riches.
verses: Wherewithall shall a yong man cleanse his way? by taking heede thereto according to thy word.
With my whole heart haue I sought thee: O let me not wander from thy Commandements.
Thy word haue I hidde in mine heart: that I might not sinne against thee.
Blessed art thou, O Lord: teach me thy statutes.
With my lips haue I declared all the iudgements of thy mouth.
I haue reioyced in the way of thy testimonies: as much as in all riches.

Psalm 119:97, 98, 99, 100, 101, 102.text: O How loue I thy Law! it is my meditation all the day. Thou through thy Commandements hast made me wiser then mine enemies: for they are euer with mee. I haue more vnderstanding then all my teachers: for thy Testimonies are my meditation. I vnderstand more then the ancients: because I keepe thy precepts. I haue refrained my feete from euery euill way: that I may keepe thy word. I haue not departed from thy Iudgements: for thou hast taught me.
verses: O How loue I thy Law! it is my meditation all the day.
Thou through thy Commandements hast made me wiser then mine enemies: for they are euer with mee.
I haue more vnderstanding then all my teachers: for thy Testimonies are my meditation.
I vnderstand more then the ancients: because I keepe thy precepts.
I haue refrained my feete from euery euill way: that I may keepe thy word.
I haue not departed from thy Iudgements: for thou hast taught me.

Psalm 11:4, 5 and 7.text: The Lord is in his holy Temple, the Lords Throne is in heauen: his eyes beholde, his eye lids trie the children of men. The Lord trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loueth violence, his soule hateth. For the righteous Lord loueth righteousnesse: his countenance doeth behold the vpright.
verses: The Lord is in his holy Temple, the Lords Throne is in heauen: his eyes beholde, his eye lids trie the children of men.
The Lord trieth the righteous: but the wicked and him that loueth violence, his soule hateth.
For the righteous Lord loueth righteousnesse: his countenance doeth behold the vpright.

Psalm 121:1-2, 3-4, 5-6, 7-8.text: I will lift vp mine eyes vnto the hilles: from whence commeth my helpe. My helpe commeth from the Lord: which made heauen and earth. He will not suffer thy foote to bee moued: he that keepeth thee will not slumber. Behold, he that keepeth Israel; shall neither slumber nor sleepe. The Lord is thy keeper: the Lord is thy shade, vpon thy right hand. The sunne shall not smite thee by day; nor the moone by night. The Lord shall preserue thee from all euill: hee shall preserue thy soule. The Lord shall preserue thy going out, and thy comming in: from this time foorth and euen for euermore.

Psalm 122:1-2, 3-4, 4-5, 6-7, 8-9.text: I was glad when they sayd vnto me: Let vs goe into the house of the Lord. Our feete shall stand within thy gates, O Ierusalem. Ierusalem is builded as a citie, that is compact together: Whither the tribes goe vp, the tribes of the Lord, vnto the testimonie of Israel: to giue thankes vnto the name of the Lord. Whither the tribes goe vp, the tribes of the Lord, vnto the testimonie of Israel: to giue thankes vnto the name of the Lord. For there are set thrones of iudgment: the thrones of the house of Dauid. Pray for the peace of Ierusalem: they shall prosper that loue thee. Peace be within thy walles: and prosperitie within thy palaces. For my brethren and companions sakes: I will now say, Peace be within thee. Because of the house of the Lord our God: I will seeke thy good.

Psalm 123:1b-2ab, 2cdef.text: Vnto thee lift I vp mine eyes: O thou that dwellest in the heauens. Beholde, as the eyes of seruants looke vnto the hand of their Masters, and as the eyes of a maiden, vnto the hand of her mistresse: so our eyes waite vpon the Lord our God, vntill that he haue mercy vpon vs. Beholde, as the eyes of seruants looke vnto the hand of their Masters, and as the eyes of a maiden, vnto the hand of her mistresse: so our eyes waite vpon the Lord our God, vntill that he haue mercy vpon vs.

Psalm 124:1b-3, 4-6, 7-8.text: If it had not bene the Lord who was on our side: nowe may Israel say: If it had not bene the Lord, who was on our side, when men rose vp against vs: Then they had swallowed vs vp quicke: when their wrath was kindled against vs. Then the waters had ouerwhelmed vs; the streame had gone ouer our soule. Then the proud waters had gone ouer our soule. Blessed be the Lord: who hath not giuen vs as a pray to their teeth. Our soule is escaped as a bird out of the snare of the foulers; the snare is broken, and we are escaped. Our helpe is in the name of the Lord: who made heauen and earth.
verses: If it had not bene the Lord who was on our side: nowe may Israel say:

Psalm 126.text: When the Lord turned againe the captiuitie of Zion: wee were like them that dreame. Then was our mouth filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing, then said they among the heathen: The Lord hath done great things for them. The Lord hath done great things for vs: whereof we are glad. Turne againe our captiuitie, O Lord: as the streames in the South. They that sow in teares: shall reape in ioy. He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtlesse come againe with reioycing: bringing his sheaues with him.
verses: When the Lord turned againe the captiuitie of Zion: wee were like them that dreame.
Then was our mouth filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing, then said they among the heathen: The Lord hath done great things for them.

Psalm 127.text: Except the Lord build the house, they labour in vaine that build it: except the Lord keepe the citie, the watchman waketh but in vaine. It is vaine for you to rise vp early, to sit vp late, to eate the bread of sorrowes: for so hee giueth his beloued sleepe. Loe, children are an heritage of the Lord: and the fruit of the wombe is his reward. As arrowes are in the hand of a mightie man: so are children of the youth. Happie is the man that hath his quiuer full of them, they shall not be ashamed: but they shall speake with the enemies in the gate.
verses: Except the Lord build the house, they labour in vaine that build it: except the Lord keepe the citie, the watchman waketh but in vaine.
It is vaine for you to rise vp early, to sit vp late, to eate the bread of sorrowes: for so hee giueth his beloued sleepe.

As arrowes are in the hand of a mightie man: so are children of the youth.

Psalm 128:1-2, 3, 4-5.text: Blessed is euery one that feareth the Lord: that walketh in his wayes. For thou shalt eat the labour of thine handes: happie shalt thou bee, and it shall be well with thee. Thy wife shalbe as a fruitful Uine by the sides of thine house, thy children like Oliue plants: round about thy table. Beholde that thus shall the man be blessed; that feareth the Lord. The Lord shall blesse thee out of Zion: and thou shalt see the good of Ierusalem, all the dayes of thy life.
verses: Blessed is euery one that feareth the Lord: that walketh in his wayes.
For thou shalt eat the labour of thine handes: happie shalt thou bee, and it shall be well with thee.
Thy wife shalbe as a fruitful Uine by the sides of thine house, thy children like Oliue plants: round about thy table.
Beholde that thus shall the man be blessed; that feareth the Lord.
The Lord shall blesse thee out of Zion: and thou shalt see the good of Ierusalem, all the dayes of thy life.

Psalm 130.text: Out of the depths haue I cryed vnto thee, O Lord. Lorde, heare my voice: let thine eares be attentiue to the voice of my supplications. If thou, Lord, shouldest marke iniquities: O Lord, who shal stand? But there is forgiuenesse with thee: that thou mayest be feared. I wait for the Lord, my soule doeth waite: and in his worde doe I hope. My soule waiteth for the Lord, more then they that watch for the morning: I say, more then they that watch for the morning. Let Israel hope in the Lord, for with the Lord there is mercy: and with him is plenteous redemption. And hee shall redeeme Israel, from all his iniquities.
verses: Out of the depths haue I cryed vnto thee, O Lord.
Lorde, heare my voice: let thine eares be attentiue to the voice of my supplications.

But there is forgiuenesse with thee: that thou mayest be feared.
I wait for the Lord, my soule doeth waite: and in his worde doe I hope.
My soule waiteth for the Lord, more then they that watch for the morning: I say, more then they that watch for the morning.
Let Israel hope in the Lord, for with the Lord there is mercy: and with him is plenteous redemption.
And hee shall redeeme Israel, from all his iniquities.

Psalm 131:1bcde, 2, 3.text: Lord, my heart is not haughtie, nor mine eyes loftie: neither doe I exercise my selfe in great matters, or in things too high for mee. Surely I haue behaued and quieted my selfe as a child that is weaned of his mother: my soule is euen as a weaned childe. Let Israel hope in the Lord, from henceforth and for euer.
verses: Lord, my heart is not haughtie, nor mine eyes loftie: neither doe I exercise my selfe in great matters, or in things too high for mee.

Let Israel hope in the Lord, from henceforth and for euer.

Psalm 132:1-2, 3-5, 11, 12, 13-14.text: Lord remember Dauid, and all his afflictions: How he sware vnto the Lord, and vowed vnto the mightie God of Iacob. Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house: nor goe vp into my bed. I will not giue sleepe to mine eyes: or slumber to mine eyelids, Untill I finde out a place for the Lord: an habitation for the mightie God of Iacob. The Lord hath sworne in trueth vnto Dauid, hee will not turne from it; of the fruit of thy body will I set vpon thy throne. If thy children will keepe my couenant and my testimonie, that I shall teach them; their children also shall sit vpon thy throne for euermore. For the Lord hath chosen Zion: he hath desired it for his habitation. This is my rest for euer: here will I dwell, for I haue desired it.
verses: Lord remember Dauid, and all his afflictions:
How he sware vnto the Lord, and vowed vnto the mightie God of Iacob.
Surely I will not come into the tabernacle of my house: nor goe vp into my bed.
I will not giue sleepe to mine eyes: or slumber to mine eyelids,

The Lord hath sworne in trueth vnto Dauid, hee will not turne from it; of the fruit of thy body will I set vpon thy throne.

For the Lord hath chosen Zion: he hath desired it for his habitation.
This is my rest for euer: here will I dwell, for I haue desired it.

Psalm 132:11, 12, 13-14, 17-18.text: The Lord hath sworne in trueth vnto Dauid, hee will not turne from it; of the fruit of thy body will I set vpon thy throne. If thy children will keepe my couenant and my testimonie, that I shall teach them; their children also shall sit vpon thy throne for euermore. For the Lord hath chosen Zion: he hath desired it for his habitation. This is my rest for euer: here will I dwell, for I haue desired it. There will I make the horne of Dauid to budde: I haue ordained a lampe for mine Anointed. His enemies will I clothe with shame: but vpon himselfe shall his crowne flourish.

Psalm 132:6-7, 8-10.text: Loe, wee heard of it at Ephrata: we found it in the fields of the wood. We will goe into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstoole. Arise, O Lord, into thy rest: thou, and the Arke of thy strength. Let thy Priestes be clothed with righteousnesse: and let thy saints shout for ioy. For thy seruant Dauids sake: turne not away the face of thine Anointed.
verses: Loe, wee heard of it at Ephrata: we found it in the fields of the wood.
We will goe into his tabernacles: we will worship at his footstoole.
Arise, O Lord, into thy rest: thou, and the Arke of thy strength.
Let thy Priestes be clothed with righteousnesse: and let thy saints shout for ioy.
For thy seruant Dauids sake: turne not away the face of thine Anointed.

Psalm 134.text: Beholde, blesse yee the Lord, all yee seruants of the Lord: which by night stand in the house of the Lord. Lift vp your hands in the Sanctuary: & blesse the Lord. The Lord that made heauen and earth: blesse thee out of Zion.
verses: Beholde, blesse yee the Lord, all yee seruants of the Lord: which by night stand in the house of the Lord.
Lift vp your hands in the Sanctuary: & blesse the Lord.
The Lord that made heauen and earth: blesse thee out of Zion.

Psalm 135.text: Praise ye the Lord, Praise ye the Name of the Lord: prayse him, O ye seruants of the Lord. Yee that stand in the House of the Lord: in the courts of the house of our God. Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good: sing praises vnto his Name, for it is pleasant. For the Lord hath chosen Iacob vnto himselfe: and Israel for his peculiar treasure. For I know that the Lord is great: and that our Lord is aboue all gods. Whatsoeuer the Lord pleased, that did he in heauen and in earth: in the Seas, and all deepe places. Hee causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth, he maketh lightnings for the raine: he bringeth the winde out of his treasuries. Who smote the first borne of Egypt: both of man and beast. Who sent tokens and woonders into the midst of thee, O Egypt: vpon Pharaoh, and vpon all his seruants. Who smote great nations: and slew mightie kings: Sihon king of the Amorites, and Og king of Bashan: and all the kingdomes of Canaan, And gaue their land for an heritage: an heritage vnto Israel his people. Thy Name, O Lord, endureth for euer: and thy memoriall, O Lord, throughout all generations. For the Lord will iudge his people: and he will repent himselfe concerning his seruants. The idoles of the heathen are siluer and golde: the worke of mens hands. They haue mouthes, but they speake not: eyes haue they, but they see not: They haue eares, but they heare not: neither is there any breath in their mouthes. They that make them are like vnto them: so is euery one that trusteth in them. Blesse the Lord, O house of Israel: blesse the Lord, O house of Aaron. Blesse the Lord, O house of Leui: ye that feare the Lord, blesse the Lord. Blessed be the Lord out of Zion; which dwelleth at Ierusalem. Praise ye the Lord.
verses: Praise ye the Lord, Praise ye the Name of the Lord: prayse him, O ye seruants of the Lord.
Yee that stand in the House of the Lord: in the courts of the house of our God.
Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good: sing praises vnto his Name, for it is pleasant.
For the Lord hath chosen Iacob vnto himselfe: and Israel for his peculiar treasure.
For I know that the Lord is great: and that our Lord is aboue all gods.
Whatsoeuer the Lord pleased, that did he in heauen and in earth: in the Seas, and all deepe places.
Hee causeth the vapours to ascend from the ends of the earth, he maketh lightnings for the raine: he bringeth the winde out of his treasuries.
Who smote the first borne of Egypt: both of man and beast.
Who sent tokens and woonders into the midst of thee, O Egypt: vpon Pharaoh, and vpon all his seruants.
Who smote great nations: and slew mightie kings:
Sihon king of the Amorites, and Og king of Bashan: and all the kingdomes of Canaan,
And gaue their land for an heritage: an heritage vnto Israel his people.
Thy Name, O Lord, endureth for euer: and thy memoriall, O Lord, throughout all generations.
For the Lord will iudge his people: and he will repent himselfe concerning his seruants.
The idoles of the heathen are siluer and golde: the worke of mens hands.
They haue mouthes, but they speake not: eyes haue they, but they see not:
They haue eares, but they heare not: neither is there any breath in their mouthes.
They that make them are like vnto them: so is euery one that trusteth in them.
Blesse the Lord, O house of Israel: blesse the Lord, O house of Aaron.
Blesse the Lord, O house of Leui: ye that feare the Lord, blesse the Lord.
Blessed be the Lord out of Zion; which dwelleth at Ierusalem. Praise ye the Lord.

Psalm 136.text: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: for his mercy endureth for euer. O giue thankes vnto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for euer. O giue thankes to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him who alone doth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him that by wisedome made the heauens: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him that stretched out the earth aboue the waters: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for euer. The sunne to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for euer. The moone and starres to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him that smote Egypt in their first borne: for his mercy endureth for euer. And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for euer. With a strong hand and with a stretched out arme: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him which diuided the red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for euer. And made Israel to passe through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for euer. But ouerthrewe Pharaoh and his hoste in the red sea: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him which led his people through the wildernesse: for his mercy endureth for euer. To him which smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for euer. And slue famous kings: for his mercy endureth for euer. Sihon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for euer. And Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for euer. And gaue their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for euer. Euen an heritage vnto Israel his seruant: for his mercy endureth for euer. Who remembred vs in our lowe estate: for his mercy endureth for euer. And hath redeemed vs from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for euer. Who giueth foode to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for euer. O giue thankes vnto the God of heauen: for his mercy endureth for euer.
verses: O giue thankes vnto the Lord, for hee is good: for his mercy endureth for euer.
O giue thankes vnto the God of gods: for his mercy endureth for euer.
O giue thankes to the Lord of lords: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him who alone doth great wonders: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him that by wisedome made the heauens: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him that stretched out the earth aboue the waters: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him that made great lights: for his mercy endureth for euer.
The sunne to rule by day: for his mercy endureth for euer.
The moone and starres to rule by night: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him that smote Egypt in their first borne: for his mercy endureth for euer.
And brought out Israel from among them: for his mercy endureth for euer.
With a strong hand and with a stretched out arme: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him which diuided the red sea into parts: for his mercy endureth for euer.
And made Israel to passe through the midst of it: for his mercy endureth for euer.
But ouerthrewe Pharaoh and his hoste in the red sea: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him which led his people through the wildernesse: for his mercy endureth for euer.
To him which smote great kings: for his mercy endureth for euer.
And slue famous kings: for his mercy endureth for euer.
Sihon king of the Amorites: for his mercy endureth for euer.
And Og the king of Bashan: for his mercy endureth for euer.
And gaue their land for an heritage: for his mercy endureth for euer.
Euen an heritage vnto Israel his seruant: for his mercy endureth for euer.
Who remembred vs in our lowe estate: for his mercy endureth for euer.
And hath redeemed vs from our enemies: for his mercy endureth for euer.
Who giueth foode to all flesh: for his mercy endureth for euer.
O giue thankes vnto the God of heauen: for his mercy endureth for euer.

Psalm 137:1-2, 3, 4-5, 6.text: By the riuers of Babylon, there wee sate downe, yea we wept: when we remembred Zion. Wee hanged our harpes vpon the willowes, in the midst thereof. For there they that carried vs away captiue, required of vs a song, and they that wasted vs, required of vs mirth: saying, Sing vs one of the songs of Zion. How shall we sing the Lords song: in a strange land? If I forget thee, O Ierusalem: let my right hand forget her cunning. If I doe not remember thee, let my tongue cleaue to the roofe of my mouth; if I preferre not Ierusalem aboue my chiefe ioy.
verses: By the riuers of Babylon, there wee sate downe, yea we wept: when we remembred Zion.
Wee hanged our harpes vpon the willowes, in the midst thereof.
For there they that carried vs away captiue, required of vs a song, and they that wasted vs, required of vs mirth: saying, Sing vs one of the songs of Zion.
How shall we sing the Lords song: in a strange land?
If I forget thee, O Ierusalem: let my right hand forget her cunning.
If I doe not remember thee, let my tongue cleaue to the roofe of my mouth; if I preferre not Ierusalem aboue my chiefe ioy.

Psalm 138:1-2, 2-3, 6, 8.text: I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise vnto thee. I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name. I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name. In the day when I cried, thou answeredst me: and strengthenedst me with strength in my soule. Though the Lord be high, yet hath he respect vnto the lowly: but the proud he knoweth afarre off. The Lord wil perfit that which concerneth me: thy mercie, O Lord, endureth for euer: forsake not the works of thine owne hands.
verses: I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise vnto thee.
I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name.
I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name.
In the day when I cried, thou answeredst me: and strengthenedst me with strength in my soule.

I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name. I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name. In the day when I cried, thou answeredst me: and strengthenedst me with strength in my soule. All the kings of the earth shall praise thee, O Lord: when they heare the words of thy mouth. Yea they shall sing in the wayes of the Lord: for great is the glory of the Lord.

Psalm 138:1-2ab, 2cde-3, 7c-8.text: I will praise thee with my whole heart: before the gods will I sing praise vnto thee. I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name. I will worship towards thy holy temple, and praise thy name, for thy louing kindnesse and for thy trueth: for thou hast magnified thy word aboue all thy name. Though I walke in the mids of trouble, thou wilt reuiue me, thou shalt stretch foorth thine hand against the wrath of mine enemies: and thy right hand shall saue me. The Lord wil perfit that which concerneth me: thy mercie, O Lord, endureth for euer: forsake not the works of thine owne hands.

Psalm 139:1-3, 7-8, 9-10, 13-14ab.text: O Lord, thou hast searched mee, and knowen me. Thou knowest my downe sitting, and mine vprising: thou vnderstandest my thought afarre off. Thou compassest my path, and my lying downe, and art acquainted with all my wayes. Whither shall I goe from thy spirit? or whither shall I flie from thy presence? If I ascend vp into heauen, thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there. If I take the wings of the morning: and dwell in the vttermost parts of the Sea: Euen there shall thy hand leade me: and thy right hand shall hold me. For thou hast possessed my reines: thou hast couered me in my mothers wombe. I will praise thee, for I am fearefully and wonderfully made, marueilous are thy works: and that my soule knoweth right well.
verses: O Lord, thou hast searched mee, and knowen me.
Thou knowest my downe sitting, and mine vprising: thou vnderstandest my thought afarre off.
Thou compassest my path, and my lying downe, and art acquainted with all my wayes.
Whither shall I goe from thy spirit? or whither shall I flie from thy presence?
If I ascend vp into heauen, thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there.
If I take the wings of the morning: and dwell in the vttermost parts of the Sea:
Euen there shall thy hand leade me: and thy right hand shall hold me.
For thou hast possessed my reines: thou hast couered me in my mothers wombe.
I will praise thee, for I am fearefully and wonderfully made, marueilous are thy works: and that my soule knoweth right well.

My substance was not hid from thee, when I was made in secret: and curiously wrought in the lowest parts of the earth.
